red_baseline.reason: T019 owns normalized CLI/MCP equivalence and the four US1 real-entrypoint journeys; implementation repair belongs to T020.
red_baseline.summary: Final RED after repairing one test-owned sentinel reference: 7/8 real-entrypoint journeys pass; task.list alone exposes CLI type/command/cwd versus MCP longRunning projection drift for T020.

validation.command: node --check <T019 test files>; npm run check
validation.summary: Both integration suites parse and the repository syntax check passes for 93 JavaScript files.
Initial run passed 6/8; one valid task.list projection mismatch and one T019-owned incorrect sentinel ID.
After the test-owned repair, 7/8 pass; the sole remaining failure is the precise task.list CLI/MCP projection mismatch owned by T020.

review.evidence: The final failure compares the frozen semantic result fields through real CLI and official-SDK stdio entrypoints and differs only in task inventory shape; all generated IDs and approved provenance differences are excluded explicitly rather than weakening semantics.

obligation_evidence.evidence: All tests traverse real CLI or MCP into the same state, run, journal, ownership, and clean authorities.
Only approved queries and low-risk mutations are exercised; medium/force/external/unknown inputs remain refusal cases.
CLI schemaVersion 1 envelopes and nested Agent results are retained and asserted before normalization.
Cross-surface reuse proves one detached managed run survives adapter boundaries and remains independently owned by Launchdeck runtime state.
The expected fixture lists the exact allowed surface differences and makes no unsupported Plugin, host, bundle, or platform claim.
Real start/run/stop/clean MCP calls and CLI commands return Kernel-shaped AgentOperationResult records.
CLI-first, MCP-first, and synchronized starts each produce one run/spawn with honest reuse effects.
External and unknown live processes remain alive after both surfaces refuse; no extra spawn receipt is created.
Old safe-clean digest returns plan_digest_mismatch and preserves safe/risky targets; adoption.apply stays absent.
Every MCP journey uses the exact registered project ID and never process cwd as authority.
Target existence drift changes the recomputed plan under locks and refuses before deletion.
Normalization compares complete outcome and resource objects independently for query, run, clean, refusal, and adoption branches.
CLI schemaVersion 1 and MCP cliSchemaVersion null are separately asserted while shared protocol/build versions must match.
Byte inventory, process receipt, and port checks prove adoption inspection has zero writes/starts and no apply action.
All MCP evidence uses the real stdio helper whose raw framing isolation remains enforced.
The expected normalization fixture excludes only generated operation IDs and approved provenance fields; task inventory shape is intentionally not normalized away.
All MCP legs use official Client/StdioClientTransport against the real server process.
